text:
Here's the grade summary I copied from the portal:

| Student | ID | Counselor | Email |
|---|---|---|---|
| Nadia Elbaz | 4482913 | Mr. D. Abernathy | nelbaz27@jhhs.org |

Can you help her plan around the B- in APUSH?
notes: PII inside markdown table cells with pipe delimiters; counselor is an unknown third party not in known_entities.

text:
SAT registration confirmation, pasting the table so you have it:

| Name | Test Center | Contact |
|------|-------------|--------|
| Daniel Okafor | Mount Rainier Lutheran High | (253) 555-0164 |

He wants a study plan for the six weeks before the test.
notes: Test center is a school name that is NOT the student's declared school; must be caught by NER, not the pseudonym map.

text:
Robotics leadership table from the club charter doc:

| Role | Person |
|------|--------|
| Captain | Priya Raghunathan |
| Co-captain | Alyssa Grover |
| Faculty sponsor | Ms. Trentini |

Priya wants the captain role to anchor her leadership spike.
notes: Two unknown third parties (peer and teacher) in table rows plus the student's bare first name in prose after the table.

text:
| Ridgeview STEM Academy — Fall Schedule (Lucas Whitfield) | | |
|---|---|---|
| P1 | AP Calc BC | Rm 118 |
| P2 | AP Physics C | Rm 204 |
| P3 | English 11H | Rm 310 |

Is three APs plus varsity soccer too much load for the fall?
notes: Name and school live inside the table header row rather than a body cell; header-row spans can be skipped by cell-wise scrubbers.

text:
From the homeroom roster sheet she photographed:

Name | Grade | Homeroom
Sofia Marquez | 11 | Rm 204 (Ms. Bettencourt)

She says Ms. Bettencourt is willing to write her a rec letter next year.
notes: Pipe-delimited table without leading/trailing pipes (loose markdown); unknown teacher appears in a cell and again in prose.

text:
Fee waiver worksheet the family uploaded:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Applicant | Ethan Kowalski |
| Mailing address | 1147 Glenview Terrace, Naperville, IL 60540 |
| Household size | 5 |

Do they qualify for the College Board waiver?
notes: Full street address inside a table value cell; regex layer must span the comma-separated address across one cell.

text:
Contact block from her portfolio README:

| Channel | Handle |
|---|---|
| GitHub | @amara.codes |
| School email | adiallo@student.northgate.org |

Amara Diallo wants feedback on whether the portfolio is strong enough to submit.
notes: Handle with a dot in it inside a table cell; naive @-word regexes stop at the dot and leak the suffix.

text:
Both kids' testing dates, from the family calendar table mom keeps:

| Student | Test | Date |
|---|---|---|
| Noah Lindqvist | ACT | Sep 12 |
| Elsa Lindqvist | PSAT | Oct 14 |

Only Noah is enrolled with us but the prep schedule has to work around Elsa's rides too.
notes: Sibling (declared family) in a parallel table row plus bare first names of both kids in the prose after the table.

text:
Intake summary for the new family:
- Student: Isabella Ferraro
- School: Brookline Latin School
- Parent contact: gferraro@ferrarodental.com
- Pediatrician on file: Dr. Anand Subramanian
Main goal is a pre-med narrative built around her hospital volunteering.
notes: Bullet-list intake form; parent's email domain leaks the family business name and the pediatrician is an unknown third party.

text:
Coach note after Tuesday's session:
- goes by "JB" with teammates, full name Jaden Brooks on all school records
- wants to quit jazz band, keep debate
- ask about summer program essay next week
He lit up talking about the mock trial win.
notes: Nickname introduced in quotes inside a bullet; the two-letter alias must map to the same pseudonym as the full name.

text:
Rec letter plan we sketched:
- Rec letter 1: Dr. Simone Achterberg (AP Bio) — strongest relationship
- Rec letter 2: Coach Terrence Mullins — can speak to grit
- Backup: counselor letter
Hana Yoshida should ask both before spring break.
notes: Two unknown recommenders in bullets with titles and parentheticals; only the student is in known_entities.

text:
Logistics for Omar Haddad's retake week:
- Chem retake Thursday 3:30
  - proctored by Mrs. Okonjo in the annex
  - mom needs a heads-up the night before
    - her cell: 312-555-0187
- No tutoring Friday
notes: Phone buried two levels deep in nested bullets; unknown proctor appears mid-list.

text:
Grace Nakamura's action items this week:
- [x] draft activities list
- [ ] email Ms. Kwiatkowski about the stats retake
- [ ] confirm shift swap with her manager Rodrigo at the bakery
- [ ] 30 min SAT reading, 3x
notes: GitHub-style checkbox list; unknown teacher plus an unknown first-name-only coworker that NER must still flag.

text:
Theo Vandenberg's visit list, from his notes app:
- tour cedar falls high on friday (transfer option)
- shadow day at his current school the week after
- ask the cedar falls ap coordinator about IB vs AP
He's leaning toward transferring.
notes: School name entirely lowercase inside casual bullets, reading like common nouns; second mention drops the word 'high'.

text:
Carpool notes for the Saturday program:
- pickup at 88 Rosemont Ave, Apt 3B at 7:40
- Leila Farrokhzad brings her cello — needs the back seat folded
- drop-off same spot by 1pm
Her mom asked us to keep this on file.
notes: Home address with apartment unit inside a bullet, no city or ZIP to anchor the address regex.

text:
Marcus Delgado's outside support, per his mom:
- private tutor Mr. Villanueva, Tuesdays
- tutor posts practice sets from @mathwithmrv on Instagram
- therapist appointment moved to Wednesdays (keep tutoring clear of it)
Fold the Tuesday sessions into the weekly plan.
notes: Unknown tutor's name and social handle in bullets; the handle belongs to a third party, not the student.
PII as JSON string values pasted into chat; counselor email is not in known_entities and must fall to the regex layer.
Nested JSON object; guardian name shares the surname and the E.164-style phone has hyphens plus a plus prefix.
Python-style single-quoted dict rather than strict JSON; scrubbers keyed to double-quoted JSON strings miss it.
District student ID with a letter prefix inside JSON, plus an abbreviated unknown advisor name.
Malformed JSON (unquoted keys, trailing comma) with a hyphenated double surname; parsers that pre-parse JSON before scrubbing will throw and may skip.

text:
His dad pasted the config from the school's club-signup bot:

name: Tomas Rybar
school: St. Aloysius Prep
slack: @trybar
team: cybersec-b

He wants the cybersecurity club counted as a STEM activity.
notes: YAML-style key:value block; the school name contains an abbreviation with a period that can split sentence-based NER.
Student's name embedded in a URL slug inside a JSON value — lowercase and hyphenated, invisible to cased NER.

text:
The Model UN roster export, verbatim:

["Declan Murphy", "Yusuf Al-Amin", "Harper Stein"]

Declan is ours; the other two are delegation partners. He wants to know if co-chairing with them counts as leadership.
notes: Bare JSON array of names; two are unknown peers, and the student's first name recurs in the prose.

text:
---------- Forwarded message ----------
From: Rebecca Chen <rchen@garveyandpartners.com>
To: counseling@lincolnhigh.org
Subject: Maya's schedule change

Hi — Maya Chen will miss 4th period Thursday for her orthodontist appointment. Please excuse her.

Can you adjust this week's plan around the absence?
notes: Forwarded-email header block; parent's work-domain email leaks her employer, and the school inbox address is not in known_entities.

text:
---------- Forwarded message ----------
From: "Patel, Nishant" <npatel@ridgeviewstem.edu>
Date: Mon, Feb 9, 2026 at 4:12 PM
Subject: Aiden — missing labs

Aiden Castellano has two physics labs outstanding. If they're not in by Friday the quarter grade drops to a C.

How do we fit the make-up labs into his week without killing the essay timeline?
notes: Email From: header with quoted Last, First name inversion; the teacher is unknown and the student's first name appears alone in the subject.

text:
Pasting the thread with his mom for context:

> On Tue, Mar 3, 2026, Abena Mensah <abena.mensah@yahoo.com> wrote:
> Kwame has been up past 1am three nights running finishing chem homework.
> Something has to come off his plate.

I agree with her. Which activity should Kwame Mensah drop?
notes: Quoted reply chain with > prefixes on every line; name and email sit inside the quote markers.

text:
The admissions rep replied to Yara's inquiry — signature pasted below:

Best regards,
Dr. Lorraine Whitcomb
Director of Admissions, Carleton College
(507) 555-0119

Should Yara Boulos follow up now or wait until her semester grades post?
notes: Email signature block with an unknown third party's name, title, and direct phone line.

text:
---------- Forwarded message ----------
From: Jerome Hutchins <jhutchins@plainsviewisd.org>
cc: m.tanaka@plainsviewisd.org
Subject: Savannah's 504 meeting

The 504 review for Savannah Hutchins is set for March 20 at 10am. Ms. Tanaka will sit in as case manager.

Dad works at the district so he forwarded this directly — please add the meeting to her plan.
notes: cc: header carries an unknown staff email whose owner is then named in the body; 504 context makes this doubly sensitive.

text:
From: Boris Antonov
Sent: Monday, January 12, 2026 8:03 AM
To: Ilya Antonov
Subject: FW: fee waiver approved

Son — waiver went through, you can register for two more sittings. Don't tell your mother I forgot the deadline the first time.

Ilya wants to know which two test dates to pick.
notes: Outlook-style From/Sent/To header block with no angle-bracket emails — names only, in header position.

text: The department chair said she wrote to her at <zoe.abara@stu.mesaverde.org> about the research seminar but got no reply. Zoe Abara swears nothing arrived — probably the spam filter. Should she re-send from a personal account, and does the seminar fit her plan?
notes: Email wrapped in literal angle brackets mid-sentence, mimicking a pasted mail client rendering.

text:
Bottom of the summer program rejection he forwarded:

--
You are receiving this because you applied to the Coastal Research Scholars Program.
This message was sent to whollenbeck04@gmail.com. Unsubscribe.

Wyatt Hollenbeck took it hard. Can we reframe the summer plan this week?
notes: Student email hidden in mailing-list footer boilerplate below a signature divider, where scrubbers often stop reading.

text: From mom's intake note: "I work nights at Sacred Heart Medical Center so I can't do calls before 2pm. We live at 2203 Fenwick Loop, Spokane — Rosa takes the 27 bus from there to school, about 40 minutes each way." Factor the commute into Rosa Ibarra's weekly load.
notes: Parent free text volunteering her employer and home address inside a quoted passage; §7 says parent free-text fields are stripped unless whitelisted.

text: Context from the dad's message: "My husband Dario is a foreman at Bellini Concrete & Paving, so summers our son could do real site work if an engineering program wants hands-on hours." Would supervised construction hours strengthen Matteo Riccio's engineering spike or read as nepotism?
notes: Parent first name used bare, plus a small-business employer whose name contains an ampersand.

text: The father wrote: "We moved from Karachi to 415 Old Mill Rd, Cranbury NJ last year, and Ayaan is still adjusting — his old school was British curriculum." How do we frame the mid-move transcript gap for Ayaan Siddiqui's applications?
notes: Address without ZIP and with two-letter state abbreviation stuck to the city; prior-country city is also identifying in combination.

text: Her mother mentioned on the call: "I'm a paralegal at Drummond, Fisk & Howe LLP downtown, so I see what a real legal career takes." She's skeptical of Chloe's mock-trial-to-law-school plan. How should we present Chloe Bergstrom's plan to win the mom over?
notes: Employer is a law firm whose name contains commas and an ampersand — easy for entity boundaries to truncate.

text: Parent free text from the check-in form: "My coworker at Genentech, Susan Palladino, said her daughter got into a great program through a science fair pipeline — can we do the same?" Evaluate whether the regional science fair is worth the time for Julian Espinoza-Katz this spring.
notes: Unknown coworker named mid-sentence between commas, plus the parent's employer — neither is in known_entities.

text: From dad's voicemail transcription: "His dad drives for Averitt Freight and is only home weekends, so weekday practice pickups fall on grandma. We're out at 96 County Road 12, Ladonia TX, nothing close by." Rural logistics need to shape Colton Pruitt's activity choices.
notes: Rural county-road address format that street-name regexes rarely cover, plus parent employer in transcribed speech.

text: Mom's note on the intake form: "Best way to reach me during the day is at the shop, 605-555-0173 (Klein's Auto Body) — my cell doesn't get signal inside." Log that as the preferred contact and draft the kickoff call agenda for Bree Two Bulls.
notes: Work phone attributed to a named small business in a parenthetical; student surname contains a space that breaks two-token name assumptions.

text: His mother's message, verbatim: "I clean offices in the Prudential building evenings, so Danilo watches his sisters until 10. He does homework after they sleep. We're near the corner of Halsted and 63rd if any program is close." Weekly plan for Danilo Reyes has to respect the caregiving hours.
notes: Home location given as a street intersection, not a numbered address; employer named by building.

text:
Please make sure the plan works for Beatriz
Almeida since her rehearsal schedule changed to Tuesday and Thursday nights starting next week.
notes: First and last name separated by a hard line break (copy-paste artifact); token-window NER sees two one-word fragments.

text:
From the OCR'd counselor letter: "...the transition team at Wood-
row Wilson High said Kiana's credits will transfer cleanly." Confirm which of Kiana Holt's sophomore credits actually map over.
notes: School name hyphenated across a line break by OCR; the receiving school is not the student's declared school.

text:
Instructions from the registrar's office: "Send the signed forms to 512 Camellia
Court, Baton Rouge, LA 70808, attention records clerk." That's actually the family's home address — they run the homeschool co-op from the house. Add the mailing task to Jonah Thibodeaux's checklist.
notes: Home address wrapped across a line break mid-street-name; framed as an institutional address, which invites under-scrubbing.

text:
Scanned emergency card came through like this:

Student: Mireille Okoye
Guardian phone: (415)
555-0182
Allergies: none

The line break is an artifact of the scan. Please put the guardian number on the kickoff checklist.
notes: Phone number split across lines after the area code; the seven-digit remainder alone is still a partial leak.

text:
The uploaded PDF renders his teacher's contact as: "reach him at f.delacroix
@stcharlesprep.org after 3pm." That's the AP French teacher who agreed to supervise Wren's independent study. Draft the outreach note for Wren Kaczmarek.
notes: Email address broken before the @ by PDF extraction; the local part alone still identifies the unknown teacher.

text:
Session transcript excerpt, raw from the auto-transcriber:

SPEAKER 1 (Nils
Ekberg): honestly the physics olympiad felt easier than the practice sets
SPEAKER 2 (coach): that's the training effect

Use this quote in his monthly narrative about growth mindset.
notes: Speaker label split across lines inside a parenthetical by the transcriber; the name never appears intact on one line.

text:
From her counselor's midyear note: "she transferred to Immaculate Heart
Academy in January and her GPA reset under their weighting system." Explain to the family how Rukmini Deshpande's two GPAs will be read by admissions.
notes: Declared school name wrapped across a line break, so the deterministic pseudonym-map string match fails and NER must catch the fragments.

text: Top of the scanned registration form reads STUDENT NAME: TESSA VANCLEEF in block capitals. Later in the same upload the counselor writes "Tessa VanCleef is on track for honors chem," and the mom's covering note just says "tessa vancleef - junior year forms attached." Please confirm the forms are complete.
notes: Same student in ALL CAPS, CamelCase surname, and all lowercase within one document; every variant must map to the same token.

text:
Transcript slice:

DEVON MCALLISTER: I bombed the practice DBQ and I don't want to talk about it
COACH: we're going to talk about it anyway

His reflection later that night said "devon mcallister, professional DBQ bomber" — he was joking, but Devon McAllister needs a rubric-based review this week.
notes: Caps speaker label, lowercase self-reference in a journal quote, and title case in prose — three casings of one student.

text: The pep-rally flyer she uploaded is headed NORTHGATE CHARTER SPIRIT WEEK, and her note underneath says "everyone at northgate charter treats spirit week like finals week." Amaya wants to skip Thursday's events to study — reasonable? This is for Amaya Broussard's weekly plan.
notes: Declared school in shouting caps on a flyer and lowercase in casual prose; neither matches the canonical map string exactly.

text: The scanned SAT admission ticket prints his contact as RPARK@GMAIL.COM, but Rowan registered it as rpark@gmail.com — same inbox. Rowan Park is worried the ticket won't scan at the test center. It will; case doesn't matter for email. Add the ticket-print reminder to his week.
notes: Same email in upper and lower case; exact-string scrub of the registered form leaves the uppercase print leaked.

text: Certificate text from the photo: "THIS HONOR IS CONFERRED UPON LUCIA FONTAINE-BARRE FOR DISTINCTION IN CHAMBER MUSIC." Lucia Fontaine-Barre wants it listed under awards, but her dad thinks chamber music dilutes the pre-med narrative. Thoughts?
notes: All-caps hyphenated double surname inside certificate boilerplate, then title case in prose; caps text defeats capitalization-cue NER.

text: His maker portfolio lists the handle as @SkylerBuilds on the header image, but in the bio text he typed "follow @skylerbuilds for build logs." Skyler Whitmore wants the channel linked from his activities list — is 4k subscribers worth featuring?
notes: Declared handle appears in two casings and embeds the student's first name; only one casing is in known_entities.

text: The return label on her mailed transcript reads GBEMISOLA ADEYEMI, 1420 SUMMIT AVE, AKRON, OH 44301 — all caps, standard postal style. In her message she wrote, "everything in Akron closes early, I mail stuff on lunch breaks." Confirm the transcript arrived before Gbemisola Adeyemi's deadline.
notes: Postal-style all-caps name and full address on a mailing label, plus the city in normal case in prose.

text: The certificate reads: "The Excellence in Civic Journalism Prize is awarded to Maya Chen of Lincoln High for her reporting on the district budget." She wants the exact citation quoted in her activities list. Does quoting it verbatim work, or should we paraphrase?
notes: Name and school embedded in quoted award boilerplate; school appears in shortened form that misses the canonical map string.

text: From the ceremony program: "The Regional Science Medal, presented by Dr. Emil Vartanian, is conferred upon Josiah Okonkwo-Bell for his work on low-cost water filtration." Josiah asked whether the presenter's name matters on the resume line. It doesn't, but the medal does.
notes: Award citation naming an unknown presenter alongside the student; passive 'conferred upon' construction separates name from verb cues.

text: The local paper covered it: "Sixteen-year-old Freya Dunmore of Sandpoint took first place in the state ethics bowl," the Bonner County Daily Bee wrote. Freya is embarrassed by the coverage but it's genuinely good material. Where does press coverage belong in an application?
notes: News-quote pattern 'Sixteen-year-old NAME of PLACE' with age adjacent to name and city; the newspaper name is a non-PII distractor.

text: Certificate wording, verbatim: "awarded to Miriam Weiss, Central High School, for excellence in Talmudic debate at the regional tournament." Note that half the schools in that league are called central high something, so the quote needs the full name to be meaningful. Add it to her honors section.
notes: School name made of common nouns quoted in citation position, with a lowercase common-noun decoy ('central high something') later in the text.

text: Letter excerpt: "The Harold P. Streeter Memorial Scholarship, in the amount of $2,500, is awarded to Giselle Fontenot in recognition of outstanding community service." She wants to know whether scholarship dollars belong on the activities list or the honors list.
notes: Scholarship title contains a full person-like name (the memorialized donor) as a distractor; only the student is seeded PII.

text: From the banquet program: "The Rotary Club of Bexley honors Anders Halvorsen (Whetstone High, Class of 2027) for 200+ volunteer hours." anders halvorsen barely mentioned it in our session — he doesn't think service hours count for anything. They do. Draft the honors entry.
notes: Award citation with school and class year in a parenthetical, city inside a civic-org name, and a lowercase repeat of the student's name.
